---
title: "Examples"
output: rmarkdown::html_vignette
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Examples}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---

```{r, include = FALSE}
knitr::opts_chunk$set(collapse = TRUE, comment = "#>", fig.align = "center")
```

This example uses European anchovy (*Engraulis encrasicolus*) in the
Mediterranean Sea. It starts after data preparation: current and future
environmental rasters have been aligned, and a continuous SDM suitability map is
available as the current reference layer. The purpose is to show how
`climniche` interprets projected climate change relative to the current realised
climatic niche, not to document an SDM workflow.

```{r}
library(climniche)

case_path <- system.file("extdata/mediterranean_anchovy", package = "climniche")
if (identical(case_path, "")) {
  case_path <- file.path("..", "inst", "extdata", "mediterranean_anchovy")
  if (!dir.exists(case_path)) {
    case_path <- file.path("inst", "extdata", "mediterranean_anchovy")
  }
}

metric_summary <- read.csv(
  file.path(case_path, "anchovy_climniche_metric_summary.csv")
)
class_proportions <- read.csv(
  file.path(case_path, "anchovy_climniche_class_proportions.csv")
)
variable_contributions <- read.csv(
  file.path(case_path, "anchovy_climniche_variable_contributions.csv")
)
layer_manifest <- read.csv(
  file.path(case_path, "anchovy_biooracle_layer_manifest.csv")
)
sdm_settings <- read.csv(
  file.path(case_path, "anchovy_presence_background_sdm_settings.csv")
)
fit_settings <- read.csv(
  file.path(case_path, "anchovy_climniche_fit_settings.csv")
)
predictor_screen <- read.csv(
  file.path(case_path, "anchovy_predictor_correlation_vif_screen.csv")
)
```

## Prepared inputs

The environmental data are Bio-ORACLE v3 surface layers. Baseline time layers
were averaged for the current rasters; future rasters use the same variables
under SSP245 at the 2050 time coordinate.

```{r}
unique(layer_manifest[, c(
  "depth", "current_time", "future_scenario", "future_time"
)])

layer_manifest[layer_manifest$retained_for_climniche, c(
  "label", "current_dataset", "future_dataset"
)]
```

The current reference layer is a continuous suitability raster from a
presence-background SDM. Background cells were sampled from analysable
Mediterranean cells outside occurrence cells; they are not confirmed absences.
The fitted suitability values above the threshold are used as continuous
reference weights.

```{r}
sdm_settings[sdm_settings$setting %in% c(
  "presence_cell_count",
  "background_cell_count",
  "background_to_presence_ratio",
  "sdm_test_auc",
  "sdm_test_tss",
  "sdm_threshold"
), ]
```

Predictor screening was completed before the `climniche` fit. The table below
records the variables retained for the niche-relative exposure calculation.

```{r}
predictor_screen[
  predictor_screen$role == "climniche exposure calculation" &
    predictor_screen$retained,
  c("label", "vif_after_screening")
]
```

## Niche-relative decomposition

`climniche` reports four related quantities, not four independent ecological
processes. Climatic Displacement is the total movement from current to future
conditions. Niche Distance Shift is the signed radial change relative to the
current realised niche centre. Climatic Reconfiguration is the remaining
non-radial component, calculated from the geometric identity
`D^2 = R^2 + C^2`. Niche Boundary Exceedance is the positive distance beyond the
weighted empirical boundary of the current realised niche.
All four quantities are dimensionless distances in the standardised,
sensitivity weighted climatic space.

```{r}
fit_settings[fit_settings$setting %in% c(
  "boundary_quantile",
  "tolerance",
  "stable_climate_change",
  "stable_reconfiguration",
  "boundary_exceedance_tolerance"
), ]

metric_summary[, c(
  "n", "reference_weight_sum", "boundary_quantile",
  "mean_climate_change_amount", "mean_niche_distance_change",
  "prop_niche_divergence", "prop_niche_convergence",
  "prop_niche_exceedance", "prop_climate_reconfiguration"
)]
```

The derived exposure classes are a secondary, hierarchical interpretation of
the continuous quantities. They are not one class per reported quantity. The
table retains all five possible classes, including classes with zero reference
weight in this example.

```{r}
class_proportions
```

Variable contributions summarise fitted changes in niche potential. They are
model-derived contributions, not causal attribution.

```{r}
variable_contributions
```

## Mediterranean maps

Panel (a) shows the continuous suitability values retained as current reference
weights. Panels (b)-(e) show the four continuous reported quantities. Panel
(f) maps the exposure classes observed in the analysed cells. Land is shown in
grey and is not part of the calculation.

```{r, echo = FALSE, out.width = "100%"}
knitr::include_graphics("figures/anchovy-mediterranean-maps.png")
```

## Summary figure

The summary figure combines the weighted Climatic Displacement plane, all five
possible class proportions, normalised variable contribution shares and
weighted distributions of the reported quantities. Zero values for a class
mean that no current reference weight was assigned to that class.

```{r, echo = FALSE, out.width = "95%"}
knitr::include_graphics("figures/anchovy-climniche-showcase.png")
```

The exposure plane separates total displacement from radial niche shift. Cells
with similar displacement can differ in whether future conditions move toward
or away from the realised niche centre, and only a subset exceeds the empirical
niche boundary.

## Reproducing the climniche step

The raster workflow needs aligned current and future environmental layers.
`occupied` can be a binary map or a continuous suitability map. With a
continuous suitability map, `occupied_threshold` removes low suitability cells
but does not convert the remaining values to one.

```r
sdm_threshold <- as.numeric(
  sdm_settings$value[sdm_settings$setting == "sdm_threshold"]
)
selected_predictors <- predictor_screen$variable[
  predictor_screen$role == "climniche exposure calculation" &
    predictor_screen$retained
]

fit <- fit_climniche_terra(
  current = current_layers[[selected_predictors]],
  future = future_layers[[selected_predictors]],
  occupied = anchovy_suitability,
  occupied_threshold = sdm_threshold,
  domain = mediterranean_mask,
  sensitivity = predictor_sensitivity,
  boundary = 0.95
)

climniche_summary(fit)
plot_climniche_map(fit, metric = "niche_distance_change",
                   occupied = anchovy_suitability,
                   occupied_only = TRUE,
                   occupied_threshold = sdm_threshold)
plot_climniche_classes(fit, occupied = anchovy_suitability,
                       occupied_only = TRUE,
                       occupied_threshold = sdm_threshold)
plot_climniche_maps(fit, occupied = anchovy_suitability,
                    occupied_only = TRUE,
                    occupied_threshold = sdm_threshold)
plot_climniche_showcase(fit)
```

The same calculation can be run from matrices after raster values have been
extracted.

```r
fit <- fit_climniche(
  current = current_values[, selected_predictors],
  future = future_values[, selected_predictors],
  occupied = suitability_values,
  occupied_threshold = sdm_threshold,
  sensitivity = predictor_sensitivity,
  boundary = 0.95
)
```
